author: NGTmeaty
text:
##### Steps to reproduce the problem:

1.mitmproxy in terminal  
2.On mobile device, attempt to put proxy in.  
3.Device cannot connect to internet

##### Any other comments? What have you tried so far?

I've tried multiple wifis, a lot of things, also other proxy programs, I'm unsure what the problem is, any help would be awesome!

##### System information

Mitmproxy version: 2.0.2 (release version)  
Python version: 3.6.1  
Platform: Linux-4.11.3-1-ARCH-x86\_64-with-arch-17.1-ISO-Rolling  
SSL version: OpenSSL 1.1.0f 25 May 2017  
Linux distro: Antergos Linux 17.1 ISO-Rolling

author: mhils
text: Do you see "clientconnect" messages in mitmproxy's event log? If not, that's a sure indicator that your device does not reach your proxy server.

author: NGTmeaty
text: Hi, there are no clientconnect messages, I can ping the server from my phone(android).

author: mhils
text: In this case there's no traffic reaching the proxy, so this is not a mitmproxy bug. You need to get your client <-> mitmproxy connection working. <http://docs.mitmproxy.org/en/stable/modes.html> might be helpful.

author: NGTmeaty
text: I'm attempting to do a regular proxy, however it isn't working, it doesn't seem as if the proxy is being made, anyway I can check?

author: ritiek
text:
[@NGTmeaty](https://github.com/NGTmeaty) You can check it running a port scan with `nmap` from another pc.

Below should work tell you if the port is open or closed:

```
nmap <local_ip_of_pc_running_mitmproxy> -p <port_being_used_by_mitmproxy>
```

author: ritiek
text:
[@NGTmeaty](https://github.com/NGTmeaty) Weird, it should give out either `closed` or `open`. `filtered` means something is wrong.

It is possible that your firewall/AV is blocking requests from devices attempting to connect to the port. Try turning them off for a while if they are on.

Also, have you tried running mitmproxy on some other port? What does `nmap` scan say before and after mitmproxy is started on the new port?
